title: Dry-Only Monotone Booster on Physical Wear Signals
summary: For normal dry running, next-lap pit hazard should usually rise as tyre-age ratio, degradation pressure, and pace loss worsen, but unconstrained tree models can learn non-physical reversals from noisy same-lap context.
rationale: For normal dry running, next-lap pit hazard should usually rise as tyre-age ratio, degradation pressure, and pace loss worsen, but unconstrained tree models can learn non-physical reversals from noisy same-lap context. LightGBM monotone constraints give a direct way to regularize that shape.
implementation_hint: Train a dry-only LightGBM expert with monotone increases on features like `TyreLife_ExpectedLife_Ratio`, `Wear_Pressure`, `LapTime_Loss`, and possibly `LapsRemaining_AfterExpectedLife`. Keep endgame/rule-pressure features either unconstrained or in a separate residual model. Select by grouped CV that keeps races separate.
expected_effect: Smoother pit-window estimates and better public-LB robustness from reduced overfit to noisy field aggregates.
risk: Wrong constraint directions will hurt, especially near finish/no-stop zones or Monaco-specific rule effects.
